title: Voltage Divider
description: Two resistors divide an input voltage proportionally. The output voltage depends on the ratio of the resistors.

steps.instruction: Observe the voltage divider. The source provides 10 V across two resistors in series: R1 = 1 kΩ and R2 = 2 kΩ.
steps.hint: The same current flows through both resistors because they are in series.

steps.instruction: The output voltage (across R2) follows the divider formula: Vout = Vin × R2 / (R1 + R2). Calculate the expected Vout.
In the Falstad simulator, right-click R2 and change it to 1 kΩ. What does Vout become?
steps.hint: Vout = 10 × 1000/2000 = 5 V. Equal resistors split the voltage equally.

steps.instruction: Restore R2 to 2 kΩ, then change R1 to 500 Ω. How does this affect Vout?
steps.hint: Vout = 10 × 2000/2500 = 8 V. Decreasing R1 raises the output voltage.
